Package intelligence

Class GaAcoIntelligence

java.lang.Object
intelligence.GaAcoIntelligence
All Implemented Interfaces:
UAVIntelligence
public final class GaAcoIntelligence extends Object implements UAVIntelligence
UAVIntelligence implementation that combines frontier-based coverage exploration with coverage-gated GA→ACO→LocalSearch path planning.

Per-tick behaviour (onTick(long, simulation.UAV, simulation.Environment))

  1. Coverage update — marks sectors within drone range as visited so that sectors traversed during EXECUTE routes are correctly recorded and not re-targeted during the next PATROL phase.
  2. Frontier steering — during PATROL mode, computes the nearest unvisited sector centre and passes it to UAV.setExplorationTarget(int, int), driving the UAV toward unexplored areas. Cleared when the entire field is covered.
  3. Coverage-gated replanning — triggers UAV.replan(List) only when all four conditions hold:
    • UAV is in PATROL mode (not mid-route).
    • KB size ≥ GaAcoConfig.getPlanningThreshold().
    • Tick is divisible by GaAcoConfig.getPlanningInterval().
    • Coverage fraction ≥ GaAcoConfig.getMinCoverageBeforePlan().

Target selection

Before calling UAV.replan(List), this intelligence:
  1. Filters the KB snapshot to untokenised nodes only.
  2. Sorts them by squared Euclidean distance from the UAV.
  3. Takes the nearest GaAcoConfig.getMaxRouteWaypoints() entries.
This compact-disc selection produces short routes (~500–800 ticks), enabling multiple replanning cycles per session and higher overall field coverage.

Ownership

This class owns a CoverageGrid instance that is exposed via getCoverageGrid() for GUI rendering and final statistics.

  • Constructor Details

    • GaAcoIntelligence

      public GaAcoIntelligence(SimulationConfig config, GaAcoConfig algConfig)
      Constructs a GaAcoIntelligence from simulation and algorithm configurations.
      Parameters:
      config - simulation parameters (field dimensions, drone range); must not be null
      algConfig - GA-ACO and planning-schedule parameters; must not be null

  • Method Details

    • onTick

      public Route onTick(long tick, UAV uav, Environment env)
      Updates the coverage grid, steers the UAV toward the nearest unvisited sector during PATROL, and triggers replanning when all conditions are met.
      Specified by:
      onTick in interface UAVIntelligence
      Parameters:
      tick - simulation tick
      uav - the drone entity
      env - the simulation environment (available for extensions)
      Returns:
      new Route if replanning fired, null otherwise

    • getLabel

      public String getLabel()
      Description copied from interface: UAVIntelligence
      Human-readable label shown in run reports and the GUI sidebar.
      Specified by:
      getLabel in interface UAVIntelligence
      Returns:
      display label, e.g. "Predefined Patrol" or "Autonomous GA→ACO"

    • getCoverageGrid

      public CoverageGrid getCoverageGrid()
      Description copied from interface: UAVIntelligence
      Returns the coverage grid managed by this intelligence, or null if this intelligence does not track field coverage.

      Used by SimulationRunner to include sector data in GUI snapshots and in the final coverage-fraction statistic.

      Specified by:
      getCoverageGrid in interface UAVIntelligence
      Returns:
      CoverageGrid instance, or null

    • interceptPoint

      public int[] interceptPoint(SensorNode target, int uavX, int uavY, int uavSpeed)
      Predictive ballistic intercept for moving sensor nodes.

      Solves the quadratic |p₀ + v·t − uav|² = (uavSpeed·t)² for the smallest positive t, then returns the predicted node position at that time. If the node is stationary or no real positive solution exists, falls back to the node's current position.

      Specified by:
      interceptPoint in interface UAVIntelligence
      Parameters:
      target - the current waypoint node
      uavX - UAV's current x position
      uavY - UAV's current y position
      uavSpeed - UAV's speed in cells per tick
      Returns:
      [x, y] of the point to steer toward; clamping to field bounds is handled by the caller
